questions.question: Explain the difference between exception handling and traditional error handling using return codes.
expectedPoints: Exception handling separates error code from normal logic
Exceptions automatically propagate up call stack
Return codes can be ignored, exceptions cannot

questions.question: Describe the RAII pattern and explain its importance in exception handling with a practical example.
expectedPoints: RAII stands for Resource Acquisition Is Initialization
Resources are managed by objects with destructors
Destructors automatically clean up resources when objects go out of scope
Example: File handler class that automatically closes files
Prevents resource leaks during exception propagation
Ensures exception safety without explicit cleanup code
